lessons.name: The ADO framework — what to actually SAY in the room
Drill: demo fails during executive presentation
Drill: client demands 6 weeks of work in 3 days
Drill: explain stochastic model variability to a CFO
Drill: client IT blocks the integration
Drill: same problem recurs 2 hours after fix
Scoping a project with a non-technical buyer
Written status cadence — the no-meeting Slack update
When (and how) to escalate to your manager
Killing a project gracefully — when the right answer is "we shouldn't build this"

existing_cards.q: ADO framework for handling an angry-customer / outage moment?
existing_cards.a: Acknowledge the impact in their words, Diagnose visibly (think out loud so they see progress), Own the resolution timeline with a concrete next checkpoint. Never blame the customer's environment in the moment.
Acknowledge impact ("I hear you") → Diagnose visibly ("checking X now, can you tell me Y?") → Own resolution timeline ("status by EOD, here's plan").
Acknowledge + run two tracks: visible (show recorded version) + backend (debug). Never blame customer's env in the moment. RCA within 24h.

existing_cards.q: Customer demands 6 weeks in 3 days — move?
existing_cards.a: Decompose to the 20% that unblocks them + document tradeoffs + get the REAL deadline (often softer than stated).

existing_cards.q: Same problem recurs 2 hours after fix — response?
existing_cards.a: Own as Sev1 + commit specific timeline + status updates every 4 hours. Defending the prior fix kills trust further.
Four labeled bullets: Shipped this week, In flight, Risks/blockers, Next week. 5 short bullets read better than 3 paragraphs; lead with outcomes, not activity.

existing_cards.q: Customer's IT team blocks integration — move?
existing_cards.a: Structured security review proposal (SOC2 docs + architecture + sub-processor list). Bypassing them = political damage.

existing_cards.q: A customer-side champion is leaving — what next?
existing_cards.a: Get reintroduced to replacement + re-confirm scope/timeline with new owner. Champion loss = retention risk.

existing_cards.q: Explaining stochastic AI to a CFO?
existing_cards.a: Weather-forecast analogy + show confidence range + 95th-percentile worst case + leading indicators. Quant always wins with CFOs.
